message:
feat: decorative policy guardrails for border, icon, and styling decisions (2dccg.10.6)

BorderTier enum + DecorativePolicy struct centralizes capability/degradation/breakpoint
policy so rendering code never makes ad-hoc decorative decisions. Also fixes pre-existing
app.rs compile issues (Line lifetime param, dead assignment).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.3","title":"Add style-system invariant tests for profiles, overrides, and semantic guarantees","description":"## Goal
Provide foundational unit-level assurance for **style-system core semantics** (profile downgrade, override precedence, and semantic token contracts).

## Scope Boundary (Canonical)
This bead owns the **core style-system contract layer** consumed by multiple tracks.
- Includes: `style_system.rs` invariants for profile downgrade behavior, environment/override precedence, and semantic token guarantee checks.
- Excludes: cross-surface rendering matrix and interaction/event routing; those belong to `2dccg.11.1` and `2dccg.11.2`.

## Work Items
- Add tests for profile downgrade behavior (`mono/ansi16/ansi256/truecolor`) and monotonic style loss.
- Add override merge + precedence tests (theme config vs env flags vs runtime options).
- Add semantic token presence/contrast guarantees for critical tokens used by pills/tabs/gutters/find bar.
- Emit invariant-oriented failure messages that identify preset/profile/token and violated contract.

## Acceptance
- Core style-system invariants are enforced by automated tests.
- Failures clearly identify violated invariant and execution context.
- Provides reusable guarantees that downstream rendering/E2E suites can build on without duplicating precedence logic.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:47:02.023999373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:08:47.873466620Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:08:47.873433278Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:47:02.023999373Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:54:02.816457171Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:53:59.204999477Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:54:01.719381666Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":336,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.6","title":"Implement decorative and border policy guardrails by capability, degradation, and breakpoint","description":"## Goal
Control decorative features and border strategy so they enhance quality without destabilizing performance, following explicit policy tied to capability detection and degradation level.

## Scope Boundary (Canonical)
This bead owns **capability/breakpoint/degradation policy mapping** for decorative and border choices.
- Includes: policy tables and enforcement for which decorative/border tiers are allowed under each capability/degradation/breakpoint condition.
- Excludes: runtime transition hysteresis/debouncing and threshold retuning ownership (`2dccg.5.2`), which this policy layer consumes.

## Phase 1: Decorative-Policy Guardrails (original 2dccg.10.6)
- Define decorative feature tiers: gradients, icons, Unicode box drawing, color depth features.
- Map each tier to minimum capability requirements (color profile, Unicode support, frame budget).
- Implement policy enforcement in degradation decision path.
- Ensure graceful stripping: decorative -> informational -> minimal.

## Phase 2: Adaptive Border Policy (absorbed from 2dccg.10.7)
- Define border strategy tiers: rounded Unicode -> plain box -> ASCII -> minimal/none.
- Connect border selection to LayoutBreakpoint (Narrow/MediumNarrow/Medium/Wide).
- Connect border selection to DegradationLevel (Full -> SimpleBorders -> NoStyling -> EssentialOnly).
- Validate border rendering at each breakpoint x degradation combination.
- Ensure border policy respects terminal capability detection (Unicode support).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Decorative elements do not appear on terminals that cannot render them correctly.
- Border strategy adapts smoothly to both viewport size and degradation level.
- Policy decisions are logged and testable via capability-matrix diagnostics.
- No hardcoded border/decorative choices bypass the policy system.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:47:22.592958338Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:05:40.547049273Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:47:22.592958338Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:54:26.765327635Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:54:22.151765326Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":339,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.9","title":"Add capability-matrix diagnostics and tests for style policy decisions","description":"## Goal
Provide strong observability and testability for style-policy decisions across terminal profiles, including user-visible diagnostics for field triage.

## Work Items
- Add diagnostics exposing key policy inputs/outputs in tests, debug mode, and user-visible status/HUD surfaces where appropriate.
- Add matrix tests for capability tiers + env-flag combinations.
- Ensure logs include scenario id, profile, applied policy, degraded layers, and why a mode/policy was selected.
- Document capture workflow so issue reports include enough runtime context for fast triage.

## Acceptance
- Style-policy behavior is explainable from logs without code spelunking.
- Matrix tests cover expected and edge-case combinations.
- Field reports can distinguish policy-driven degradation from implementation bugs.
- Failures report actionable context.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:47:30.430840611Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:58:48.959639574Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:58:48.959615930Z","close_reason":"Absorbed into 2dccg.5.3 (degradation + capability tests unified)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.9","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:47:30.430840611Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.9","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:54:19.687640160Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.9","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:54:48.798402801Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.9","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:54:48.958555740Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":327,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Merged overlap from coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.4: diagnostics scope now covers both test/debug observability and user-visible degradation/capability triage surfaces, with documented capture expectations.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:41:41Z"},{"id":397,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ABSORBED into 2dccg.5.3 — capability-matrix diagnostics are the testing counterpart of degradation regression tests.  {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","title":"Track E: Recalibrate degradation policy to preserve essential styling","description":"## Objective
Tune degradation behavior so terminals with sufficient capability keep meaningful styling rather than prematurely collapsing into bland output.

## Why This Exists
A suspected contributor to the quality drop is over-aggressive degradation logic tied to frame budget/terminal capability that strips colors/backgrounds too early.

## Scope
- Audit degradation decisions in render pipeline
- Define minimum visual hierarchy that should survive moderate degradation
- Adjust thresholds/policy and validate against representative terminal profiles

## Deliverables
- Documented degradation policy table (capability -> retained style layers)
- Updated heuristics/thresholds or guards in code
- Regression checks for no-style / low-style / full-style scenarios

## Completion Criteria
- Capable terminals retain tabs/pills/role hierarchy consistently
- Low-capability terminals remain readable without broken contrast
- Behavior is deterministic and testable (not ad-hoc per frame)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:47.279389078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:28.544781336Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:47.279389078Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:38:30.698611175Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":253,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Track E Intent
FrankenTUI’s degradation system is powerful, but if tuned too aggressively it can erase the very hierarchy we worked to build.

### Strategy
Audit first, then tune. Avoid blind threshold changes. Preserve readability and hierarchy while keeping low-capability terminals functional.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:41:40Z"}]}

Code-path map
1. Runtime budget profile is configured in `src/ui/app.rs:12753-12760` using `FrameBudgetConfig { total: 120ms, allow_frame_skip: false, degradation_cooldown: 12, upgrade_threshold: 0.25, ..relaxed() }`.
2. Render-time degradation gates are read in `src/ui/app.rs:11615-11637` (`frame.degradation`) and projected into four booleans: `use_unicode_borders()`, `render_decorative()`, `apply_styling()`, `render_content()`.
3. Those gates directly control:
   - border type fallback (`Rounded` -> `Square`) at `src/ui/app.rs:11619-11623`
   - whether pane/modal borders render (`Borders::ALL` vs `Borders::NONE`) at `src/ui/app.rs:11626-11630`
   - whether semantic styles are applied vs plain monochrome at `src/ui/app.rs:11636-11647` and related style slots
   - whether search query inset styling is shown at `src/ui/app.rs:11804-11815`
   - whether pane content body is rendered (analytics/sources/modal content paths) at `src/ui/app.rs:12138-12358`
4. Degradation-level semantics come from ftui in `ftui-render/src/budget.rs:621-730`:
   - `Full`: unicode borders + styling + decorative + content
   - `SimpleBorders`: unicode off, styling/decor/content still on
   - `NoStyling`: unicode off, styling off, decor/content on
   - `EssentialOnly`: unicode off, styling off, decorative off, content on
   - `Skeleton`: content off (layout shells only)
   - `SkipFrame`: max degradation

Observed stripping matrix (current behavior)
- `Full`: no strip
- `SimpleBorders`: rounded/box-drawing affordance downgraded to square/ascii-like look
- `NoStyling`: all semantic color/attribute hierarchy collapses to plain style (chips/tabs/gutters cues flatten)
- `EssentialOnly`: borders/decorative chrome removed (`Borders::NONE`) while content remains
- `Skeleton`: content text suppressed in gated paths; pane shells persist
- `SkipFrame`: max level; in this app frame-skip is disabled (`allow_frame_skip=false`), but visual output remains at effectively skeleton/no-content behavior where gates apply

Cross-check with tests
- Enum progression + gate semantics validated in `src/ui/app.rs:16452-16479`.
- Border suppression expectation validated in `src/ui/app.rs:17129-17152`.

Candidate over-aggressive flattening contributors for follow-up (`2dccg.5.2` / `2dccg.10.6`)
- Hard style collapse at `NoStyling` removes semantic differentiation too early for capable terminals under transient pressure.
- `render_decorative()` gate currently disables all borders at `EssentialOnly`, which can reduce scannability in dense panes.
- Decision thresholds for controller/e-process are largely inherited from ftui defaults (`ftui-render/src/budget.rs:342-357`, `454-481`) and should be revalidated against cass workload characteristics.

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.2","title":"Tune degradation thresholds and smooth transitions for capable terminals","description":"## Goal
Rebalance degradation so essential hierarchy survives on capable terminals, and avoid abrupt visual cliffs when degradation level changes at runtime.

## Scope Boundary (Canonical)
This bead owns **runtime degradation transition dynamics**.
- Includes: threshold tuning, hysteresis/debouncing, and smooth level-to-level transition behavior.
- Excludes: capability-to-feature policy mapping for decorative/border strategy (`2dccg.10.6`), which consumes these tuned transition dynamics.

## Phase 1: Threshold Tuning (original 2dccg.5.2)
- Review current degradation trigger points (frame time, memory, resize frequency).
- Identify which style layers are most impactful for readability vs purely decorative.
- Raise thresholds where terminals can handle richer rendering without instability.
- Validate that tuned thresholds don't cause flickering or oscillation.

## Phase 2: Smooth Transitions (absorbed from 2dccg.10.8)
- Add intermediate states between degradation levels to prevent jarring visual jumps.
- Implement hysteresis or debouncing so level changes settle before committing.
- Ensure style layers fade/simplify gradually rather than vanishing all at once.
- Preserve key hierarchy markers (bold headers, role gutters, active-tab indicators) across transitions.
- Test transition smoothness under rapid resize and theme-switch scenarios.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Capable terminals preserve full-fidelity rendering under normal workload.
- Degradation transitions are smooth enough that users don't notice level changes.
- No oscillation between levels under stable conditions.
- Key information hierarchy survives at every degradation level.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:37.221785028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:12:07.088227364Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:57:44.059146259Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:37.221785028Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:45.759251758Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":359,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.3","title":"Add degradation and capability-matrix regression tests with diagnostic fixtures","description":"## Goal
Guard against future over-degradation and provide strong observability for style-policy decisions across terminal profiles.

## Scope Boundary (Canonical)
This bead owns **policy-level degradation and capability-matrix regression coverage**.
- Includes: degradation-level retention/stripping invariants, capability profile expectation tests, policy diagnostics.
- Excludes: cross-surface visual matrix aggregation (`2dccg.6.3`) and full user-journey playback ownership (`2dccg.11.3`/`2dccg.11.7`).

## Phase 1: Degradation-Mode Regression Tests (original 2dccg.5.3)
- Create test fixtures for each DegradationLevel (Full, SimpleBorders, NoStyling, EssentialOnly).
- Test that key UI affordances (pills, tabs, role gutters, find bar) remain functional at every level.
- Verify that information hierarchy (headers > body > metadata) survives degradation.
- Assert specific style properties preserved/stripped at each level boundary.
- Test interaction behavior (keyboard nav, mouse clicks) is unaffected by degradation.

## Phase 2: Capability-Matrix Diagnostics (absorbed from 2dccg.10.9)
- Define capability matrix: color profile x Unicode support x frame budget x terminal type.
- Implement diagnostic output showing which style policies are active for a given capability set.
- Create test fixtures for common terminal profiles (xterm-256color, screen, dumb, Windows Terminal, kitty).
- Add tests that verify style-policy decisions match documented intent for each profile.
- Provide machine-readable diagnostic output for debugging unexpected visual behavior.
- Validate that NO_COLOR, CASS_RESPECT_NO_COLOR, and TERM override behavior is consistent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A regression that over-strips styles at any degradation level is caught by tests.
- Each capability profile has documented expected behavior and tested invariants.
- Diagnostic output enables fast triage of 'why does it look wrong on terminal X?' reports.
- Tests remain fast enough for regular local runs (< 5s for full matrix).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:44.553982074Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:05:25.822343510Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:58:26.534372521Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:58:23.248572736Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:36:44.553982074Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:46.292623674Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":360,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:

Explicit include/exclude contracts now separate component-level, surface-level, policy-matrix, and cross-cutting verification responsibilities so parallel execution is safer and less duplicative.","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:04:10Z"},{"id":432,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation update (2026-02-08) — threshold tuning + transition smoothing baseline

What changed
- Added a dedicated cass runtime budget profile helper: `cass_runtime_budget_config()` in `src/ui/app.rs`.
- Switched runtime startup to consume that helper in `run_tui_ftui()` instead of ad-hoc inline values.

New profile values
- `total = 120ms` (unchanged high-level quality-first target)
- `phase_budgets = { diff: 6ms, present: 12ms, render: 24ms }` (explicitly scaled relative to default 2/4/8)
- `allow_frame_skip = false` (unchanged)
- `degradation_cooldown = 20` (up from 12)
- `upgrade_threshold = 0.40` (up from 0.25)

Why this addresses 5.2
- Higher cooldown + higher upgrade threshold reduce level-flapping under transient pressure.
- Explicit phase budgets make overrun signals less opaque and align better with the 120ms frame target.
- Frame-skip remains disabled to avoid abrupt visual disappearance.

Tests added
1. `cass_runtime_budget_profile_is_quality_first_and_stable`
   - Asserts the full profile contract above.
2. `cass_runtime_budget_upgrade_is_debounced_by_cooldown`
   - Asserts upgrades are blocked until cooldown frames elapse, then allowed under strong headroom.

Validation run
- `cargo fmt --check` (pass)
- `cargo check --all-targets` (pass)
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` (pass)
- `cargo test cass_runtime_budget_profile_is_quality_first_and_stable -- --nocapture` (pass)

Note on targeted second test execution
- `cargo test cass_runtime_budget_upgrade_is_debounced_by_cooldown -- --nocapture` currently collides with pre-existing unrelated `src/ui/style_system.rs` unit-test import errors (`super::app::LayoutBreakpoint`) in this shared branch context; those errors are outside this bead’s changed codepath.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T22:30:22Z"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","title":"Track F: Rich fixtures + snapshot coverage for visual regression protection","description":"## Objective
Upgrade test fixtures and snapshot coverage so the UI quality we care about is actually exercised by automated tests.

## Why This Exists
Current snapshots use minimal synthetic content that under-represents real-world visual complexity, allowing regressions in styling, hierarchy, and affordances to slip through.

## Scope
- Create richer fixture data (multi-agent roles, tool calls, markdown/code blocks, long snippets, score spread)
- Add snapshot variants covering pills, tabs, detail view, and role gutters
- Ensure tests are deterministic and cheap enough for regular local/CI runs

## Deliverables
- Expanded fixture builders/data helpers
- New snapshot baselines for key visual surfaces
- Test docs clarifying how to regenerate snapshots when intentional UI changes happen

## Completion Criteria
- Visual regressions in key surfaces produce obvious snapshot diffs
- Fixtures represent realistic cass workloads (not toy text-only lines)
- Snapshot updates are deliberate and reviewable","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:56.403007457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:28.796092040Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:56.403007457Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:38:30.594645265Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":254,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Track F Intent

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.3","title":"Add targeted cross-theme and cross-degradation visual regression matrix","description":"## Goal

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","title":"Add search-surface regression tests (structure, interactions, and visual hierarchy)","description":"## Goal
Protect search-surface rendering with tests that verify structure, interactions, and visual hierarchy remain correct after polish changes.

## Scope Boundary (Canonical)
This bead is the **single owner** of search-surface regression coverage.
- Includes: search bar, filter pills, breadcrumbs, footer HUD, and search-input affordance behavior.
- Excludes: results-surface regression ownership (`2dccg.9.5`), global interaction state-machine routing ownership (`2dccg.11.2`), cross-theme/degradation matrix aggregation (`2dccg.6.3`), and full workflow E2E playback (`2dccg.11.3`).
- Must reuse baseline fixtures/helpers from `2dccg.6.2` instead of duplicating baseline snapshot setup.

## Test Scenarios

### Structure Tests
- Search bar renders with mode-aware title and cursor position.
- Filter pills render with correct style tokens (STYLE_PILL_ACTIVE for active, standard for inactive).
- Breadcrumb trail renders with clickable segments and correct emphasis.
- Footer HUD renders with KBD key/desc token styling.
- Layout respects LayoutBreakpoint constraints (search bar width, pill wrapping).

### Interaction Tests
- Tab key cycles through filter pills in expected order.
- Enter key on search bar triggers search.
- Click on pill toggles active/inactive state.
- Escape key clears search input or exits mode.
- Mouse hover on interactive elements shows correct cursor affordance.

### Visual Hierarchy Tests
- Active filter pills have higher visual weight than inactive.
- Search bar input area is visually distinct from surrounding chrome.
- Footer key hints maintain KBD/desc pairing across themes.
- Theme switch updates all search-surface styles immediately.

## Logging
- Each test case logs: test_id, component, action, expected_state, actual_state.
- Failures include terminal size, active theme, degradation level.
- Use structured logging from `2dccg.11.6` when available.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All search-surface regressions from the ftui migration are caught.
- Tests run in < 3s total and require no terminal (headless).
- Test names clearly indicate which search-surface element is being validated.
- Suite composes with `2dccg.6.2`/`2dccg.6.3`/`2dccg.11.x` without duplicate ownership.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:46:15.642528388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:05:32.127079571Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:16.298074887Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:46:15.642528388Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:51:36.523677893Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:51:40.892002824Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:51:46.311618406Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:52:05.096187123Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":373,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.8.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution contract for this task:

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9","title":"Track I: Results-surface richness + motion parity restoration","description":"## Objective
Rebuild results-pane visual density, hierarchy, and kinetic feedback so high-volume result scanning feels premium rather than flat.

## Why This Track Exists
The results surface is the highest-frequency user touchpoint. Legacy FTUI rehab work identified regressions in grouped pane structure, snippet richness, score/source cues, density tuning, and animation reconnect.

## Scope
- Pane chrome/focus clarity
- Snippet rendering quality and wrap budgets
- Score bars/source cues/remote tint/striping hierarchy
- Reveal/focus animation state wiring with budget guardrails
- Compact/cozy/spacious density tuning

## Deliverables
- Faster cognitive scan of result lists
- Strong visual prioritization of relevance/context
- Performance-safe motion and density behavior

## Completion Criteria
- Results-pane rubric dimensions hit target threshold
- No frame-budget regressions caused by richer rendering
- Regression tests lock visual structure across densities","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:45:42.073119807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:38.831253253Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:45:42.073119807Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:49:37.792307379Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":384,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track orchestration note:
